name: Amur Leopard
description: One of the rarest big cats in the world with fewer than 70 individuals remaining. Population crisis caused by habitat loss and illegal hunting.

name: Vaquita
description: The world's smallest porpoise. Only about 10 left due to illegal fishing nets and accidental capture in fishing equipment.

name: Javan Rhino
description: Fewer than 70 individuals survive in one protected reserve. Hunted for horn and habitat destruction.

name: California Condor
description: These massive vultures nearly went extinct. Once reduced to just 27 birds but slowly recovering through careful breeding programs.

name: Saola
description: Called the Asian unicorn but critically endangered. Fewer than 250 left due to deforestation and hunting.

name: Sumatran Orangutan
description: Forest habitat destroyed for palm oil plantations. Population cut by over 80 percent in the last 20 years.

name: Mountain Gorilla
description: Found in volcanic forests threatened by human expansion and civil conflict. Population recovering slowly to about 1000.

name: Axolotl
description: This aquatic salamander is nearly extinct in the wild due to water pollution and habitat loss in Mexico City.

name: Bornean Elephant
description: Smallest elephant subspecies. Habitat destruction from logging and palm oil farming has reduced population to under 1500.

name: Kakapo
description: Flightless parrot from New Zealand with fewer than 200 surviving birds. Only found on predator-free islands now.
